title: Tulja Bhavani Chalisa
intro: Shri Tulja Bhavani Chalisa: Full Path, Meaning, and Benefits - Hymn to Goddess Tulja Bhavani
description:
|| Shri Tulja Bhavani Chalisa ||

|| Doha ||
Namo namo Jagadambike Mata
Charankamal raj sevak raata
Sansar paar utarihen tum
Bhaktan ke dukh bhar vihata

|| Chaupai ||
Jai jai Jagadambike sukhdayi
Tulja Bhavani Amba mai
Jata mukut sir shobha pavai
Chandra birajat tilak man bhavai
Trishul hast kar mein dhari
Dusht dalan par kripa tumhari
Sinh vahini tuhi savari
Karahu kripa Jagadambike bhari
Ved Puran mein tumhin samai
Mahima apar kahi na jaai
Trigun vasini tuhi Bhavani
Kripa karahu mamata sukhdani
Asht bhuja dharini Mata
Sharnagat pale vihata
Kalratri ko tumhin samani
Bhaktjanan ki ho hitkari
Shubhra vasan par shobha pave
Hast kamal kamal man bhave
Dhanya tumharo ye roop vichari
Karta harai bhav bandhan hari
Tulja Bhavani devi pyari
Karahu sahay Ambe hamari
Bhaktan par kar kripa bhari
Sankat harahu bhav bandhan tari
Saptashringi tuhi Vaishnavi Mata
Bhaktjanan ke dukh harta
Prem sahit jo dhyan lagaye
Sankat hare kripa barsaye
Vishnu ki maya tuhi Bhavani
Karahu sahay Jagadambike mai
Trigun vasini tuhi Tripurari
Vishwanath ki ho adhikari
Sarva siddhi ki ho adhikari
Sharnagat ki ho uddhari
Sachche man se jo koi dhyave
Bhakti shakti sab pave
Dusht dalan kar kripa karahu
Bhavsagar se paar karahu
Sharnagat ki ho raksha
Mata Bhavani daya drishta
Namo namo Jagadambike Mata
Jai jai jai Jagadambike Mata
Jai jai Jagadambike Mata
Jai jai jai Jagadambike Mata

|| Doha ||
Shri Tulja Bhavani Ambe
Charan sharan kijai
Bhaktan par kripa karahu
Bhav bandhan har lijai
meaning:
|| Shri Tulja Bhavani Chalisa ||

|| Doha ||
Namo namo Jagadambike Mata
(Salutations again and again to Mother Jagadambike.)
Charankamal raj sevak raata
(This servant is devoted to the dust of your lotus feet.)
Sansar paar utarihen tum
(You are the one who will ferry us across this worldly ocean.)
Bhaktan ke dukh bhar vihata
(You are the remover of the burden of devotees' sorrows.)

|| Chaupai ||
Jai jai Jagadambike sukhdayi
(Victory to you, O Mother Jagadambike, the giver of happiness.)
Tulja Bhavani Amba mai
(O Mother Tulja Bhavani, O Mother Amba.)
Jata mukut sir shobha pavai
(The crown of matted hair on your head looks beautiful.)
Chandra birajat tilak man bhavai
(The moon and the tilak on your forehead are pleasing to the mind.)
Trishul hast kar mein dhari
(You hold a trident in your hand.)
Dusht dalan par kripa tumhari
(Your grace is upon those who destroy the wicked.)
Sinh vahini tuhi savari
(You are the rider of the lion.)
Karahu kripa Jagadambike bhari
(O Mother Jagadambike, shower your immense grace upon us.)
Ved Puran mein tumhin samai
(You are pervaded within the Vedas and Puranas.)
Mahima apar kahi na jaai
(Your glory is infinite and cannot be described.)
Trigun vasini tuhi Bhavani
(O Bhavani, you reside within the three Gunas.)
Kripa karahu mamata sukhdani
(O Mother of compassion, giver of happiness, shower your grace.)
Asht bhuja dharini Mata
(O Mother, you are the bearer of eight arms.)
Sharnagat pale vihata
(You sustain those who seek your refuge.)
Kalratri ko tumhin samani
(You are equal to the form of Kalratri.)
Bhaktjanan ki ho hitkari
(You are the benefactor of the devotees.)
Shubhra vasan par shobha pave
(You look beautiful in white garments.)
Hast kamal kamal man bhave
(Your lotus-like hands are pleasing to the mind.)
Dhanya tumharo ye roop vichari
(Blessed is your form when reflected upon.)
Karta harai bhav bandhan hari
(You are the remover of the bonds of worldly existence.)
Tulja Bhavani devi pyari
(O beloved Goddess Tulja Bhavani.)
Karahu sahay Ambe hamari
(O Mother Ambe, please help us.)
Bhaktan par kar kripa bhari
(Shower your immense grace upon your devotees.)
Sankat harahu bhav bandhan tari
(Remove our troubles and ferry us across worldly bonds.)
Saptashringi tuhi Vaishnavi Mata
(You are the Saptashringi and Vaishnavi Mother.)
Bhaktjanan ke dukh harta
(You are the remover of the sorrows of devotees.)
Prem sahit jo dhyan lagaye
(Whoever meditates on you with love.)
Sankat hare kripa barsaye
(You remove their troubles and shower grace.)
Vishnu ki maya tuhi Bhavani
(O Bhavani, you are the illusion of Lord Vishnu.)
Karahu sahay Jagadambike mai
(O Mother Jagadambike, please help us.)
Trigun vasini tuhi Tripurari
(You are the dweller of three Gunas and Tripurari.)
Vishwanath ki ho adhikari
(You are the consort of Vishwanath.)
Sarva siddhi ki ho adhikari
(You are the possessor of all Siddhis.)
Sharnagat ki ho uddhari
(You are the savior of those who seek your shelter.)
Sachche man se jo koi dhyave
(Whoever meditates on you with a sincere heart.)
Bhakti shakti sab pave
(They attain all devotion and power.)
Dusht dalan kar kripa karahu
(Destroy the wicked and shower your grace.)
Bhavsagar se paar karahu
(Ferry us across the ocean of worldly existence.)
Sharnagat ki ho raksha
(You are the protector of those who seek refuge.)
Mata Bhavani daya drishta
(O Mother Bhavani, keep your merciful gaze upon us.)
Namo namo Jagadambike Mata
(Salutations again and again, O Mother Jagadambike.)
Jai jai jai Jagadambike Mata
(Victory, victory, victory to Mother Jagadambike.)
Jai jai Jagadambike Mata
(Victory to Mother Jagadambike.)
Jai jai jai Jagadambike Mata
(Victory, victory, victory to Mother Jagadambike.)

|| Doha ||
Shri Tulja Bhavani Ambe
(O Shri Tulja Bhavani Ambe.)
Charan sharan kijai
(Please accept me in your shelter.)
Bhaktan par kripa karahu
(Shower your grace upon your devotees.)
Bhav bandhan har lijai
(Please remove the bonds of worldly existence.)

faq.q1: What are the main benefits of reciting the Tulja Bhavani Chalisa?
faq.a1: Maa Tulja Bhavani is considered the Goddess of Power (Shakti). Reciting this Chalisa grants the devotee immense self-confidence, courage, and mental strength. It is also helpful in dispelling negative energies.
faq.q2: Which is the best day to recite it?
faq.a2: Tuesdays and Fridays are considered extremely auspicious for worshiping Maa Bhavani. Additionally, reciting it during the nine days of Navratri is especially rewarding.
faq.q3: Can it be read at home?
faq.a3: Yes, Maa Tulja Bhavani is the protector of her devotees' homes. You can recite it at your home shrine with cleanliness. Visiting a temple is not mandatory.
faq.q4: Does the Tulja Bhavani Chalisa provide protection from enemies?
faq.a4: Maa Bhavani is the 'destroyer of evil.' Reciting the Chalisa with sincere devotion and a pure heart protects devotees from enemies and adverse situations.
faq.q5: Is there a specific time designated for this?
faq.a5: Brahma Muhurta or the evening (Sandhyakal) are considered the best times, but if you are busy, you can recite it at any time with a pure mind.
